entry.title: Instance for Data Attribute Recommendation cannot be created
entry.content:
Hello, wanted to try out Data Attribute Recommendation in SAP Hana Trial account. In December it was working fine, but when I tried again in February, Instance creation is stuck. Creating service Data Attribute Recommendation takes a long time, about a day, after which an error occurs that the wait time has been exceeded.

Steps to recreate:

  1. Created SAP Hana trial.
  2. Created AWS US-east subaccount.
  3. Used either Booster to set up account for Data Attribute Recommendation or created service manually (tried with different Runtime Environments, and each time subaccount was deleted and created anew)
  4. After near a day long wait, error occurs about waiting time being exceeded

entry.title: Best Practice for Handling Multiple SAP Backends in UI5 Apps Without Hardcoding URLs
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently working on SAP Fiori / UI5 applications that communicate with multiple SAP backend systems via OData, and I’m looking for best practices to avoid hardcoding URLs or system-specific destinations in the app configuration.

Our system landscape contains several backend systems, for example:

  • Production: F51, F52, F53
  • Development / Sandbox: D5W, S5W

Previously, in an on-premise setup, we handled this using system aliases. In UI5 apps or destinations, we referenced something like:

 

destination: D5W_Principal_Propagation

The alias/destination pointed to the appropriate backend system, so we did not need to hardcode real URLs.

 

Current Challenge

With BTP destinations, we want to completely avoid hardcoding URLs in ui5.yaml or in the application code. However, in our scenario:

  • There are multiple backend systems per environment
  • Each system may expose its own OData services
  • Our apps previously relied on alias/destination names to choose the target system

Example of current configuration:

 

backend:
  - path: /sap
    url: http://................................
    destination: D5W_Principal_Propagation

This is DEV-specific and would require manual changes for other systems — which we want to eliminate.

 

Options I’m Considering

 Create one destination per backend system

Example: EWM_F51, EWM_F52, EWM_D5W, etc.

→ The app selects the destination dynamically based on context

 Use one destination per environment and let ABAP/Gateway system aliases handle routing

→ UI5 app calls one logical destination (for example EWM_MAIN)

→ Backend decides which system (F51/F52/F53) processes the request

 Use external configuration to avoid hardcoding destination names in the UI5 app

 

Questions

  • What is the recommended best practice when you have multiple backend systems in the same environment?
  • Is it better to create multiple destinations or rely on backend alias routing?
  • What are best practices for dynamic destination selection in UI5 apps?
  • How do you avoid hardcoding URLs and destination names in large multi-system landscapes?

Any guidance, architecture recommendations, or real project experience would be very helpful.

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP MDG-M: Duplicate Check not working despite correct Match Profile and CR configuration
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am a beginner in SAP MDG and I'm currently trying to set up my first Duplicate Matching profile for Material Master (MM) using HANA Search. I want the system to trigger a duplicate warning during the Change Request "Check" or "Submit" step if the Material Description and Material Group match an existing record. However I dont get a popup and even if I press "Check" finds nothing at all.

I set the threshold and the duplication is checked inside the MATL01 Change Request 00 process. Here are some images:

I also read that for generic duplication check using DQM I need data services, but I didnt see anything like that in the turorials Ive watched. When accessing the Connector Administration Cockpit (ESH_COCKPIT), I see the following error: "Current system is a copy of another system" and the search object connector list is completely empty.

My questions:

1. Does the Duplicate Check in MDG require the ESH Search Object Connectors to be active/fixed, even if manual HANA search works?

2. Do I need SAP Data Services (DQM) for this basic HANA-based matching? (help.sap mentions it here: Preparing Data Services to Work With MDG for Generic Duplicate Check | SAP Help Portal, but I’m trying to use native HANA capabilities).

Any help or guidance would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Deciphering the Functional Mechanics of FYV.
entry.content:
Date: February 2026

Author:-  Er. Pratik Das (B.Tech, Mechanical Engineering , SAP Functional Consultant ).

Specialization :- MM, SCM Integration & Business Process Optimization.

Target Audience: SAP Functional Consultants (MM,FI), Solution Architects, Financial Controllers & Business Process Owners (BPO), SAP S/4HANA Migration Leads & etc.

In the SAP ecosystem, the Fiscal Year Variant (FYV) is far more than a simple calendar setting; it is the temporal foundation upon which all transactional integrity rests. For a Material Management (MM) Consultant, the FYV dictates the lifecycle of a Material Document, the calculation of moving average prices, and the strict boundaries of period-end closing via MMPV.

When we configure a system for a global rollout—balancing the Western (K4) and Indian (V3) statutory requirements—precision in OB29 is non-negotiable. Misconfiguration here doesn't just result in a ledger error; it can freeze a warehouse’s ability to post Goods Receipts (GR) or Issue Goods (GI).

  1. The Functional Mechanics of FYV :- 

The Fiscal Year Variant defines the number of posting periods in a fiscal year and the number of special periods. While the standard is 12 posting periods, SAP allows up to 16 periods in total.

  • Posting Periods: These correspond to the operational months where day-to-day procurement and inventory movements occur.
  • Special Periods (13–16): These are strictly for Finance (FI). As an MM consultant, it is vital to remember that logistics movements cannot be posted to special periods. These are reserved for audit adjustments, tax corrections, and year-end closing entries that do not affect physical inventory quantities.
  1. Calendar-Dependent vs. Year-Dependent: The Implementation Choice
  2. Calendar-Dependent (The "Set and Forget" Model)

Used predominantly in Western regions (Standard Variant K4), this configuration assumes the fiscal year is identical to the Gregorian calendar (January 1 to December 31).

  • Consultant Insight: When "Calendar Year" is flagged in OB29, the system ignores the "Period" and "Day" sub-tables. It automatically knows January has 31 days and is Period 01. This is the lowest-maintenance path for a Greenfield implementation.
  1. Year-Dependent (The High-Precision Model)

A variant is "Year-Dependent" if the start and end dates of the periods change every year.

  • The 4-4-5 Retail Cycle: Common in US/UK retail, where months are defined as cycles of weeks to ensure every quarter has exactly 13 weeks. Here, "Month 1" might end on January 28th one year and January 29th the next.
  • Configuration Necessity: Every year, the consultant or the power user must manually maintain the period end-dates for the upcoming year. Failure to do this before the clock strikes midnight on New Year's Eve will result in a global "Period not defined" hard error for every Purchase Order (PO) and GR.
  1. Geographical Logic: Western (K4) vs. Indian (V3)

The primary differentiator in global rollouts is the Year Shift. This is a displacement factor ($+1$, $0$, or $-1$) that aligns the calendar year with the financial year.

The Western Style (K4)

The fiscal year matches the calendar year.

  • January: Period 01 / Shift 0
  • December: Period 12 / Shift 0

The Indian Style (V3)

The Indian fiscal year starts in April. This creates a technical overlap that requires a displacement shift.

  • April to December: These months are part of the current calendar year. Year Shift = 0.
  • January to March: These months belong to the next calendar year but the current fiscal year. To bring them back into the current fiscal year's reporting, we apply a Year Shift = -1.

Consultant Trap: If you are configuring a new Indian Company Code and forget the $-1$ shift for Jan/Feb/March, the system will try to post a March 2026 invoice into Fiscal Year 2026 instead of 2025, throwing the entire Balance Sheet out of equilibrium.

  1. Case Study: Shortened Fiscal Year During Corporate Realignment

The Scenario:

An Indian subsidiary of a German conglomerate is migrating its legacy systems to the global SAP S/4HANA template. The Indian entity currently follows V3 (April–March), but the German parent company mandates K4 (January–December) for global consolidation starting January 1st, 2026.

The Solution: The "Transition Variant"

You cannot simply change the variant in OB37 mid-year; doing so would corrupt the Material Ledger history.

  1. Define the Shortened Year: In OB29, create a new Year-Dependent variant (e.g., Z9).
  2. Period Compression: For the transition year (2025), you define only 9 periods (April 2025 to December 2025).
  3. The Cutover: On December 31st, 2025, the "Year 2025" ends.
  4. The Re-assignment: In the new year, the Company Code is assigned to the standard K4 variant.

Critical MM Checklist for this Transition:

  • MMPV: Ensure the last period of the shortened year (Period 09) is closed correctly.
  • Purchase Orders: Any open POs with delivery dates in the "missing" months (Jan-March) must be reviewed to ensure the new period logic doesn't block the GR.
  • Physical Inventory: A full wall-to-wall count is recommended at the end of the shortened year to "cleanse" the valuation before the new cycle begins.
  1. Integration Points for the MM Consultant

While FI defines the variant, the MM consultant must manage the operational impact:

  • T-Code OMSY: This is where we see the link between the Company Code and the Fiscal Year. It shows the current, previous, and last period of the previous year.
  • T-Code MMPV: This is the "Period Closer." It checks against the FYV to move the "Current Period" forward. If your FYV says Period 12 ends on March 31, but you try to run MMPV on March 30, the system will prevent the shift.
  • Material Valuation: SAP stores material stocks and values by period. If the FYV is changed without a proper migration strategy, the historical stock tables (MARCH, MARDH) will become inconsistent, leading to severe audit failures.

Conclusion

The Fiscal Year Variant is the heartbeat of SAP. Whether you are managing a standard K4 setup or a complex Year-Dependent shortened year for a merger, the logic remains the same: align the calendar to the business cycle through the Year Shift.

entry.title: Blend Data in SAP Analytics Cloud Stories
entry.content:
Introduction

The SAP Analytics Cloud is a self-service solution, which means that story designers regularly face the task of displaying data from various sources (datasets or models) in a single visualization. In this blog, I would like to show how this can be implemented. The reason I wrote this blog is that blending for tables was gone in optimized stories. Since it is back now with the new table experience I would like present it to you as might not have heard of it, as Blending was removed from SACS21 then. If you don’t want to read the whole blog you can register for the one our Live Session Exploring Data Blending in SAP Analytics Cloud Stories Exploring Data Blending in SAP Analytics Cloud Stories

The data presented in this blog is based on HANA live data sources, which are available in the Learning Hub's practice system, so everything demonstrated here can be tried out by yourself. Let’s start with it.

In reporting, data from different data sources is regularly displayed side by side so that the key figures from these data sources are visible at a glance. The data from these sources is often related to each other, as they use the same dimensions.

In both tables shown here, the same dimension Region is displayed. It makes sense to present this data in a single table so that the two key figures # of Orders and Sales Revenue are nicely placed next to each other and can be easily compared. Of course it would be dangerous to just make the two tables look like one table, because if data is changing only in only place and not in the other you might end up comparing the wrong figures. To get it straight we really need to tell SAC that a dimension in one model based on same or almost same members in the other model.

This is done via the feature "Link Dimensions." Here, the dimension that is available in both data sources needs to be linked.

It may also be desirable to create a calculation whose formula uses key figures from both data sources. In the following table, both dimensions are linkrf and a calculation has taken place. The column Sales Revenue per Order is exactly such a calculation. Another scenario would be to add an attribute which available only in one data source, but not in the other.

Basic Procedure

In order to display the data from both models in a single table, two steps must be carried out:

  1. Linking the two models via one or more common dimensions
  2. Adding a linked model in the table or chart that is supposed to display the data from both models.

To 1. Linking the two models via a common dimension

  1. In the story's edit mode, the linking is done in the data area using the button
  2. In the Link Dimensions window, you select the dimensions that have matching values. In my simple example, it is sufficient to set the dimension Region for both models, i.e., on the left and right, and confirm the settings with the Set button.




However, there may also be cases where two or more dimensions need to be linked, for example, if year and month are separate dimensions and the data should be displayed at the month level.

To 2. Adding a linked model in the table or chart that is supposed to display the data from both models.

If you now add a table or a chart in the story, you can now assign a linked model in the builder section of the designer, i.e., where the table structure is defined, for example:

Here I now select my linked model PACIFICA_SHIPPING_INFO:

Once the model is assigned, objects from both models can be included in the table structure. Here, for example, as seen in the row area:

The icon in front of PACIFICA_ORDER_FINANCE indicates that Region is a linked demension and the dot icon after Region indicates that Region is coming from the primary model.

The table with Sales Revenue from PACIFICA_ORDER_FINANCE and # of Orders from the model PACIFICA_SHIPPING_INFO now looks like this:

Finding the right (collection of) dimension(s)

It can happen that the data from two models cannot be properly combined, because the wrong dimension was linked. If there is a problem with the correctness of the displayed data for a dimension the visualization would not be rendered at all and therefore would look like this:

As you can see there is an indicator for the problem (!). If you click on it you will see this:

You will need to put Store dimension the chart.

In this case it is not sufficient to link on Region and therefore must be done at a more granular level, which is to link the Store dimension. Since here a Store is always assigned to exactly one Region, the link on Region can be removed without causing any limitations or incorrect values.

As soon as Store in the left model is linked with Store in the right model, this message disappears and instead another message appears. This time, it is the first of the two messages, which gives me a hint that the linking via Store is still not correct.

Sometimes visualization cannot be rendered, as there is a lack of uniqueness in the values. In those cases, where for example you have several stores with the same name, uniqueness is achieved by linking not via the description (default setting), but via the ID:

When linking dimension please consider:

  • In the example presented, the data of two models were linked together, but it is also possible to link three or more models. However, this increases complexity, and it is naturally worth thinking about whether to integration of the data on the model level which means before creating the story. If would do so you would get a better reusability and a better performance in most cases.
  • Two HANA Live data sources were linked together. Not all Live data sources can be linked, and here I refer to the help.sap.com. There are further restrictions related to the blending functionality, including displaying totals, Top N, sorting, creating variances based on combined calculations. A complete list of these restrictions can also be found in the official product documentation.
  • If SAC reports that values are not correctly linked, it can sometimes be very tedious to find out exactly where the problem lies. Comparing cumulative calculations with and without linking can help identify which data records are affected.

Link Types

When a linked model has been stored in a table or chart, the link type can be specified. Here you can determine how to handle cases where members of the Region dimension exist in only one of the two models.

The link type has three options:
All primary data,
All data,
Only overlapping data






Primary data:

Region

 Sales Revenue 

APJ

 $      146,326,784

China

 $        63,750,577

EMEA North

 $  1,077,635,472

Secondary data:

Region

# of Orders

APJ

1691

China

739

NA

3743

Result for the option All Primary data. Here, only the regions from the primary data are listed:

Region

 Sales Revenue 

 # of Orders

APJ

 $      146,326,784

1691

China

 $        63,750,577

739

EMEA North

 $  1,077,635,472

 

Result for the option All data. Here, all regions are listed, regardless of whether they come from the primary or secondary data, and there are empty cells for both key figures:

Region

Sales Revenue

# of Orders

APJ

 $              146,326,784.00

1691

China

 $                63,750,577.00

739

EMEA North

 $          1,077,635,472.00

 

NA

 

3744

Result for the option Only overlapping data. Here, only the regions that are present in both the primary and secondary data are listed. There are no empty cells here:

Region

 Sales Revenue 

 # of Orders

APJ

 $      146,326,784.00

1691

China

 $        63,750,577.00

739

If you ever worked with relational databases you might already have noticed that link types are similar to joins in an SQL select script. All Primary data can be understood like a Left Outer Join, All data behaves like an full outer join and Only overlapping data like an Inner Join.

Where to find additional information about Blending

  • Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/7b9defa34dc34c0881dee863e6b4e5c5.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=LATEST&q=blending
  • Blending in Future Live Session:
    I cover blending functionality in a future Live Session. This session it covers also filtering and calculations when models are linked
    https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/exploring-data-blending-in-stories-in-sap-analytics-cloud

Practice System in order to recreate the examples shown in the blog

There is so much to explore in the area of blending than I can possibly cover here, as mentioned in the beginning, all the examples here can be recreated in several SAP Learning Hub practice systems.

These are:

  • Hands-on Practice for Exploring SAP Analytics Cloud

SACE11

https://learning.sap.com/practice-systems/introduction-to-sap-analytics-cloud

  • Hands-on Practice for SAP Analytics Cloud: Story Design

SACS21

https://learning.sap.com/practice-systems/introduction-to-sap-analytics-cloud

  • Hands-on Practice for SAP Analytics Cloud: Modeling and Data Transformation

SACM21

https://learning.sap.com/practice-systems/sap-analytics-cloud-data-modeling

  • The practice systems for planning courses like SACP23, and SACP34 can be used also without any restrictions.

The two models I used In my examples are:

  • P00M_Pacifica_Order_Finance
  • P00M_Pacifica_Shipping_Order

They can be found in the following folders:

  • Public/SACE11/Content or
  • Public/SACS21/Content

Relevance for SAP Certification C_SAC

Please keep in mind that, in a way, this topic is relevant for the certification Data Analyst Certification (C_SAC) also. Although blending might not be a topic will not be asked about in the certification as it is today, but blending functionality is sure is needed in environments where multiple datasources are used in single stories and it might come back into certification again in the future. Then you would be happy to know how the functionality works.

entry.title: Re: How to import existing on-premises application from A...
entry.content: Hello, thanks for your answer. We used to work with this method in our 9.4 system version and never had any issues downloading BSP UI5 apps. Now that we’re working on an S/4HANA system, we’re facing a new problem: when using /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_LOAD, the download fails with a code page conversion error referring to a *-dbg.controller.js file — even though this debug file doesn’t actually exist in the BSP app. In S/4, the repository only contains the minified .js and .js.map files, and it seems the system tries to generate the -dbg.js version during download, which triggers the CP1252 conversion error. There’s also no ZIP download option available in this system. Has anyone encountered this behavior in S/4HANA UI5 apps, and is there a recommended workaround for downloading or modifying these controller files? Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Is it possible to create invoices from an invoicing plan without good receipt - self billing
entry.content:
Hello members,

We are using partial invoicing plan and we are planning to using periodic invoicing plan for some of our purchase order having for the same amount to be paid each month (ex a monthly subscription for a software).

I would like to do know if it is possible in a standard way to to do self billing by creating directly in our SAP the monthly invoice to be, without needing any good receipt.

I have seen the possibility with ERS, however this solution work based on the good receipt what we want to avoid. And recurrent postings in accounting doesn't allow to make a link with the purchase order so it doesn't suit neither. I have also seen that in public cloud version 2508 a new tile "manage supplier recurring invoices" exist but we are in private version so this solution won't be available for us before months/years.

So do you please know if there is another way to do it ?

I have in mind the functionality in leasing contracts which allows to generates automatically the documents to be paid to the leaser (similar to an invoice)

Thank you in advance for your help

Bring your real-world questions and scenarios to our interactive sessions on February 19 and 26, designed to provide deeper insights into product, positioning, and delivery.

 

📅 February 24: Unlocking Value: SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors Deep Dive & Value Journey
Don’t miss this focused Presales session featuring:

  • A deep dive into the SmartRecruiters product, highlighting key features and enhancements
  • A walk through of the Interactive Value Journey, gaining practical talking points to guide customer conversations

 

🛠️ Implementation Track: Key Dates to Know
Preparing for certification? Mark these key milestones:

  • February 27 – Implementation course (THR104) becomes available on SAP Learning
  • March 6 – Certification (C_THR104) becomes available on SAP Learning
  • March 10 – Need2Know Live Session: Implementation Essentials – Guidance and leading practices from our SAP Global Recruiting Consultants (SuccessFactors & SmartRecruiters) to support successful implementations (registration coming soon!)
  • March 11 – Live Session: Overview of the requirements for SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors certification – Certification overview and preparation guidance, including key knowledge areas, sample questions, and study resources

 

Whether you're just getting started or finalizing your prep, this sequence will help you build knowledge, reinforce key concepts, and approach certification with confidence.

 

🌍 Looking Ahead: 2026 SAP Partner Events

With regionally focused events and dedicated partner versions at SAP flagship events, there’s an option for every partner. Review the 2026 calendar and check back for registration updates.

entry.title: Re: Dump MOVE_TO_LIT_NOTALLOWED_NODATA in SAP CRM side
entry.content: Although I don't understand why the dump concerns the overwriting of a protected field", probably STRING leads to the dump so why don't you remove it and define a CHAR field instead? NB: in SAP_BW 7.58, the same code is moved to the subroutine CMXS_DATA_MAPPING and I see that it doesn't handle neither STRING nor XSTRING (in the loop, <fs> remains assigned to the previous field so it may assign for instance the string to a numeric field, so failing), and no SAP patch found to fix it. If it's the same kind of code in your SAP_BW version, then you may contact SAP Support.

entry.title: Code Page Conversion Error When Downloading UI5 BSP Apps in S/4HANA (CP1252 Issue)
entry.content:
Hello everyone, and thank you in advance for your support.

We previously worked with the 9.4 system version and used /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_LOAD to download UI5 BSP applications without any issues. Now that we are working on an S/4HANA system, we are facing a new problem during the download process.

When we try to download a BSP UI5 app, the report throws the following error:

Code
 
An code page conversion error occurred (path: controller/Worklist-dbg.controller.js / code page: Cp1252)
Warning: Text file ... has not been downloaded

In S/4HANA, the UI5 repository contains the minified .js files along with their .js.map source maps. During the download, the system appears to generate the corresponding *-dbg.js debug versions, and this step triggers a CP1252 code page conversion error, even though the system and SAP GUI are both Unicode. There is also no ZIP download option available in this S/4 environment.

Has anyone encountered this behavior in S/4HANA UI5 apps? Is there an SAP‑recommended workaround for downloading or modifying UI5 controllers when the system generates debug files that cannot be converted?

Thank you for any insights.

entry.title: What’s New in My Home SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 Release?
entry.content:
My Home already serves as a centralized entry point for business users by providing immediate access to key insights, supporting the efficient management of tasks and situations, and enabling users to organize their favorite pages and apps. This helps streamline daily operations and reduces the time spent navigating across the system. The 2602 release builds on this foundation by enhancing how information is delivered, personalized, and consumed—ensuring that users can focus on what matters most.  

The 2602 release of My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces a range of new delivered and beta features, along with meaningful UX enhancements designed to elevate the overall user experience. In this blog, we’ll explore what’s new in this release and how these updates help business users work more efficiently and stay better informed. 

 

Mandatory News – Ensuring critical information is seen by all business users  

With the 2508 release, administrators gained the ability to deliver timely and targeted updates directly to business users through the My Home centralized news feed. The 2602 release takes this a step further by introducing mandatory news articles. News articles containing essential company information can now be marked as required reading. Business users must acknowledge these articles, confirming that they have read and understood the content. This feature plays a critical role in ensuring compliance with legal, safety, and policy-related requirements. It also helps eliminate knowledge gaps by ensuring that all employees receive the same information. During periods of change—such as the rollout of new policies or process updates—mandatory reading ensures that users understand both the reasons for the change and its potential impact, improving alignment across the organization. 

 An example of a news article that is required reading. Alt Text: A screenshot of My Home with a pop-over “Required Reading” that is also highlighted with “Contains Critical News”. The news article has an image at the top, followed by text showing the first part of the article. The “Done” button is greyed out, the “Remind me later” button is visible.

 

The Manage News app has a toggle to mark articles as “Required Reading”. Alt Text: Screenshot of the Manage News app with the toggles set to “Yes”, and some news text in the Description field.

 

News and Pages visibility - A more focused experience 

In the 2602 release we introduced enhanced customization options, allowing business users to control the visibility of news and pages within My Home. This enables users to tailor their workspace according to their role, priorities, and daily responsibilities. By showing only relevant information and tools, My Home becomes more focused and clutter-free, helping users stay productive and work more efficiently without unnecessary distractions. 

My Home with enhanced personalization, showing News deselected and Pages selected. Alt Text: A screenshot of My Home, with eight cards in one row in the Pages section, below the ToDos section showing one situation and the Apps section.

 

My Home Settings pop-over with the new selectors for News and Pages within the News and Pages section. Alt Text: A screenshot showing the Layout part of My Home Settings, where users can select or deselect sections. For the News and Pages section, News is deselected, Pages are selected.

 

Improved Mobile Experience with horizontal scrolling 

For business users on the move, the My Home mobile experience has been enhanced with horizontal scrolling. This update supports visual browsing and improves the discoverability of tasks, apps, and insight cards on smaller screens. As a result, mobile users can navigate content more intuitively and access important information quickly—wherever they are. 

 

AI-assisted Content Addition - Adding apps using natural language. 

My Home enables you to do your job with speed and agility. Even more so now that our 2508 Beta AI – Assisted content addition feature is now available in our 2602 release as a premium feature.  Business users can now add applications using natural language in two clicks. The business user can review and verify the results generated by AI ensuring accuracy.  This saves time by dramatically simplifying the process of adding new applications and tiles to My Home. Now with this new AI assisted feature,  there is no longer a need for business users to discover the appropriate application or even to navigate away from their home page to the application and manually apply filters to find the information they require — thanks to Generative AI, the relevant information can be surfaced quickly and effortlessly using natural language. This significantly reduces the effort needed to discover and add Insights Cards. 

AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home for Applications. Alt Text: The image shows three screenshots: at the top an extract of My Home showing the blue salutation bar, with an AI button “Add Content” on the right. An arrow has been added linking it to the second screenshot, where a popover “Add Content” is shown, where the user has entered “I want to monitor sales orders what app should I use to do this”, and two of four results are listed and visualized: “Track Sales Orders” and “Manage Sales Orders” (shown as an insight tile). Below that a screenshot of the outcome in My Home: the former is added to Apps Favorites, the latter to Insights Tiles.

 

Smart Helpers - Automating Repetitive Tasks 

Business users now have access to the Beta  AI – assisted task automation in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 release. It allows key users and business users to automate their daily activities in SAP’s Fiori apps by using generative AI to create so call “smart helpers”. Those “smart helpers” execute the repetitive activities on behalf of the business user either from the SAP Fiori apps while always keeping the business user in the loop for decisions on data changes.  Business users simply describe their desired business outcome in natural language, related to a specific SAP Fiori elements-based application, and the smart helper will take care of it for you. 

Beta: The planned way of giving users an overview of the AI-assisted task automation smart helpers in My Home. Alt Text: Screenshot of My Home with a section “Smart Helpers” above the “Apps” section, with two tabs “Smart Helpers (4)” and “Active Runs (3)”. The latter is selected, showing three cards with title, description and executed step shown: one is completed, two are running.

 

Beta: Manage Smart Helpers application for defining instructions for the planned AI-assisted task automation. Alt Text: Screenshot showing the instruction text at the top, an empty section for attachments, and the execution plan with four steps: read order requests, analyze request data, simulate pending requests, create sales orders.

 

AI-assisted Smart Solution for situations – Simplifying workflow 

The Beta AI-assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home simplifies workflows by dynamically adapting the user interface to guide business users through resolution steps using the power of generative AI. Key information and actions are surfaced though dynamic cards reducing the need for in-depth navigation.  One click resolution of situations directly from the homepage thereby enhancing efficiency. There is support for bank messages for alerts, reaching project end dates when a project stage is not completed and sales quotation expiry situations. 

Beta: AI-Assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home. Alt Text: An image with two screenshots: the upper one show the top part of My Home showing five Situation cards in the To-Dos section. One titled “End Date for Project Digital Media Assessment for Media Corp…” is linked with an arrow to an expanded card shown in the second screenshot – the card is taller and wider, contains a summary on the left with two action buttons “Assign To Me” and “Close Situation”, and more details on the right.

 

Final Thoughts 

The My Home 2602 release demonstrates our continued focus on usability, personalization, and effective communication. From mandatory news articles that drive accountability to enhanced customization and mobile usability, these improvements help ensure that My Home remains a powerful and user-centric entry point to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 Beta AI-assisted features such as Beta AI-assisted Smart solution for situations and Smart Helpers are now available for Beta Testing with the 2602 release. At SAP, we’re committed to delivering the best experience possible, which is why we’re inviting you to join our Beta Testing Program. By participating, you’ll get early access to these features, have the chance to share your feedback, and help us fine-tune them before the official launch. Your input will play a key role in shaping the quality and usability of these new capabilities.  

Also, If you are interested in hearing about the latest innovations in SAP Cloud ERP, please join our Public Cloud Practitioner Forum in which SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers share best practices and provide feedback to the SAP development teams about new functionality.

entry.title: Inbound, Outbound, Certificate Bound: Certificate Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Certificates are key to secure integrations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but their roles often cause confusion. This post clarifies the different certificate types and shows how to manage them using three key SAP Fiori apps.

Introduction

Navigating certificate terms like TLS, client authentication, and certificate mapping can be a challenge at first. Fortunately, understanding their usage in S/4HANA Cloud makes it easier to secure connections and authenticate users or systems. We'll cover:

  • Certificates in S/4HANA Cloud: their purpose and types
  • How S/4HANA Cloud acts as both client and server
  • Which SAP Fiori apps to use to manage certificates

Certificate Basics

For a detailed explanation of what certificates are and how they work, see this blog post: Beyond Basic (1): Certificate-Based Authentication. Here, we just recap the main usage types.

TLS Server Certificates: Know Who You Called

TLS (Transport Layer Security) is a widely used internet standard for encrypting and validating connections between systems. It’s not unique to S/4HANA Cloud—almost all web communications rely on TLS for encryption and to confirm that the client is connecting to the intended server.
When a client initiates a connection, it checks the remote server’s TLS certificate against a list of trusted certificates (more specifically: it checks the certificate and compares the issuer of the certificate against a list of trusted issuers).

Client Certificates: Know Who Called You

Once the connection is secured by TLS and the client has verified the server’s identity, the next step is for the server to confirm that the client is authorized to connect. This authentication process works similarly to traditional user/password logins, but instead of a password, the client presents a certificate. This certificate-based method is a more secure way for the server to verify the client’s identity.

Client and Server Roles in Communication

In any communication, there’s a client (the caller) and a server (the responder). The client always checks the server’s identity via a TLS certificate, and the server can verify the client’s identity through a client certificate.

S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can act as either client or server, depending on whether it’s making outbound connections or accepting inbound ones. Depending on the role the S/4 system takes, it either needs to present a client certificate to authenticate itself to the remote server (when acting as a client), or verify the client certificate of the incoming call (when acting as a server).

Three Maintenance Apps for Certificates

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, there are three key Fiori apps to manage certificates:

  • Maintain Certificate Trust List: List and manage trusted certificate authorities (CAs) for outbound server validation
  • Maintain Client Certificates: Store and export certificates for outbound client authentication
  • Maintain Communication Users: Map inbound certificates to communication users for secure access

The maintained certificates are then referenced in the Communication Systems app and the Communication Arrangements app.

Outbound Server Validation (TLS server certificates)

When S/4HANA Cloud connects outbound, it validates the remote server’s certificate against the Certificate Trust List. The Manage Certificate Trust List app lets you add and manage trusted Certificate Authorities (CAs)—the issuing authorities of the server’s certificate.
Good to know: every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant comes with an SAP-managed list of trusted CAs (similar to the pre-delivered trust list in your browser). So most of the time, you don’t have to do much here.

As explained above, this TLS server validation is not related to authenticating specific users; it’s simply a prerequisite for establishing a secure connection.

Outbound Client Authentication

When S/4HANA Cloud connects to another system as a client, it may present a certificate for authentication. These certificates are stored in the Maintain Client Certificates app.
Good to know: every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant comes with an SAP-managed client certificate preinstalled. But you can of course create your own.

The specific certificate to be used for a connection is then referenced in the Communication Systems app under “Users for Outbound Communication” and is then selected in the communication arrangement for the specific outbound integration.

Inbound Certificate Mapping

External systems connecting to S/4HANA Cloud present their certificates, which are mapped to communication users. The user mapping happens in the Maintain Communication Users app. There you upload the client certificate (just the public key!) of the caller. This maps the communication user to the subject and issuer attributes of the client certificate.

Important to know:

  • the client's certificate is not “pinned”, so rotating the certificate is possible without re-upload (unless the issuer or subject change, of course).
  • only client certificates signed by specific CAs are accepted by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The list of these CAs cannot be changed but is predetermined by SAP and available in SAP Note 2801396.  In particular: this list is not the one maintained in the Manage Certificate Trust List app.

Conclusion

Grasping the various certificate types and their roles is crucial, though it can be complex. The main factor in successfully handling certificates is understanding your communication scenario—whether your system operates as a client or a server. This knowledge guides you in choosing the appropriate app for configuring the correct settings, ensuring your communication environment remains secure.

entry.title: Re: How can I post one receivable and one payable in the...
entry.content:
Dear @Dylan2,

You’re hitting message F5080 because your journal entry has different Consolidated company / Trading Partner values across line items:

  • The intercompany receivable line customer for affiliated company carries Trading Partner = DE2200 derived from the BP master
  • The employee payable line has Trading Partner = blank
  • With document type SA as currently configured, SAP requires the Trading Partner to be unique in the document or it inherits one value across G/L items so DE2200 vs blank triggers the error

What you can do in Public Cloud:

Option 1. Recommended

Post as two documents clean accounting + clean consolidation this avoids mixing an intercompany trading partner line with a non-intercompany line in one FI document

This keeps intercompany reporting/elimination consistent and avoids TP inconsistencies. SAP check is exactly about TP consistency

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3220073

Option 2.

If you must post both in one journal entry use a document type configured for Inter-company posting. SAP explicitly states that the Trading Partner behavior is controlled by two flags in CBC ID 101522 Define Document Types

 

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3060863

Best Practice: Don’t change SA globally copy SA to a new Z* document type for this special posting

Recommended setting for your case:

  • Inter-company = ON
  • Enter Trading Partner = blank/disabled so TP stays derived from master data per line and does not have to be unique

Then repost using the new document type

IMPORTANT: If you set both flags ON, TP is entered manually per G/L line item and TP doesn’t have to be unique either but that’s usually for cases where you want manual control on G/L lines

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SII tip for Spain #8: New Field for Hydrocarbons from Tax Deposits - February 2026
entry.content:
The Spanish Tax Agency has introduced a new field in the Suministro Inmediato de Información (SII) system to support enhanced reporting requirements for hydrocarbon transactions. This update affects the Issued Invoices Record Book (Libro registro de Facturas expedidas) and is regulated under Order HAC/27/2026, modifying Order HFP/417/2017.

In this post, the details of the new SII field and the customizing behind will be explained.

 

Introduction

Spain's Immediate Supply of Information  (SII, Suministro Inmediateo de Información) continues to evolve to meet regulatory requirements and enhance tax transparency. The latest regulatory update introduces a specialized field to identify invoices documenting the delivery of fuel from Tax/Bonded warehouses to the extractor company provided that a VAT payment guarantee evidence (including advance payments) is provided, as defined in the section eleven of the annex of the Spanish VAT Law (LIVA).

This change was formally published on January 22, 2026, through Order HAC/27/2026, following a public hearing process that began in December 2025.. Additonally, the Tax Agency has also published the updated XSD schemas, available through the developer’s portal:

  • SuministroInformacion.xsd
  • RespuestaConsultaLR.xsd

 

Details of the new SII Field <HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito>

The Spanish Tax Agency has added a new optional field to the Issued Invoices Record Book within the SII XML structure. This field serves as an identifier to flag specific hydrocarbon transactions.

  • <RegistroLRfacturasEmitidas>
    • <FacturaExpedida>
      • <HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito>
      • <TipoDesglose>
        • <DesgloseFactura>
          • <Sujeta>
            • <Exenta>
            • …

This new Identifier can contain the following values:

  • “S” (Yes) - The invoice documents hydrocarbon delivery from tax/bonded warehouse after the relevant VAT payment evidence was produced by the extractor company.
  • “N” (No) or empty (default) - The invoice does not document such transactions

Technical hints

The former customizing activity “Assign Additional Attributes to Input Tax Codes” is renamed to “Assign Additional Attributes to Tax Codes”, and will now contain the information for incoming VAT (Capital Goods) and for outgoing VAT (Fuels from Deposits)

In case the tax code is marked as “Fuels extracted from deposits”, the new tag <HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito> will be rendered in the XML as “S”. Otherwise it will not be rendered.

It is important to mention that since this tag is at the same level that the group <TipoDesglose>, it will refer to the full invoice: in case of invoices with several lines, it will only take one tax code marked as “Fuels extracted from deposits” to generate the new tag<HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito>, even in the case that other tax codes are not relevant for fuels extractions.

XML example:

 

 

In case of Contract Accounting (FICA), it is also possible to populate this value via "Change Invoice Details" Business Add-In (BAdI) implementation

 

 

 

 

Timeline

  • S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, expected by March 12, 2026
  • S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and ECC: Check the following SAP notes:
    • SAP Note 3710148 - Electronic Documents for Spain SII HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito tag Legal Change: Proxy Objects
    • SAP Note 3710149 - Electronic Documents for Spain SII: HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito tag Legal Change is on Expert Review
    • SAP Note 3701288 - SII Spain - Contract Accounting: Inclusion of Tag HidrocarburosExtraidosDeposito in Electronic Documents (FICA)

 

Related Links

SAP remains committed to supporting customers through these regulatory changes, providing timely updates and comprehensive documentation to ensure smooth transitions and continued compliance. For technical questions or implementation assistance, please contact SAP Support or consult the relevant SAP Notes and documentation:

  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in ERP

Disclaimer

This blog post is intended for informational purposes only and reflects a simplified configuration approach based on current legal and system understanding. It does not constitute legal or implementation advice. SAP system behavior may vary depending on version, scope, and customer-specific settings. For full compliance and implementation support, please consult your SAP implementation partner or legal advisor.

entry.title: Calculating IBP Technical Week “Key Figure Date” in S/4HANA Using CDS View
entry.content:
When extracting data from S/4HANA into SAP IBP, a standard calendar week may span across two months.
IBP splits such weeks into “technical weeks”, and many key figures require the first date of the technical week.
while working on custom IBP integration for transactional data (key fugures), I discovered that S/4HANA does not provide this date natively.
So I created a very simple CDS view to derive it using the I_CalendarDate standard view.
 
Example week:
  • 29-Apr-2024 → 03-May-2024
  • Crosses April → May
  • IBP interprets this as:
    • Technical Week A: 29–30 April → Key Figure Date: 29-Apr
    • Technical Week B: 01–03 May → Key Figure Date: 01-May

The CDS view below calculates this value automatically for any date.

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'IBP : Get Key Figure Date for any Date'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity ZI_IBP_Key_Figure_Date
  as select from I_CalendarDate
{
  key CalendarDate,

  @EndUserText.label:     'Key figure Date'
  @EndUserText.quickInfo: 'First day of the IBP Technical Week'
  case
    when cast( WeekDay as abap.int4 ) < cast( CalendarDay as abap.int4 )
    then dats_add_days( CalendarDate, -cast( WeekDay as abap.int4 ) + 1, 'INITIAL')
    else dats_add_days( CalendarDate, -cast( CalendarDay as abap.int4 ) + 1, 'INITIAL')
  end as KeyFigureDate
}

Result :

 

This CDS view can be used for :

  • Custom IBP extractors (CPI‑DS or RFC-based)
  • Time-based aggregation logic in Custom CDS views for IBP

Hope this will be helpful.

Regards,

Bharat Bajaj

entry.title: 🇵🇱 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Warsaw, Poland)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday 23 April 2026 in Warsaw, Poland  from 12:00 - 18:00 GMT+1. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Location: KMD Poland, Inflancka 4a, 00-189 Warszawa.

The instructor is Andre Fischer

Here are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

The event is kindly being hosted by KMD Poland, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Mastering Scheduling Agreements in SAP S/4HANA: Highlights to Long-Term Procurement Excellence
entry.content:
Scheduling Agreements – SAP S/4HANA

Overview

A scheduling agreement is a long-term procurement arrangement between a company and its suppliers that establishes predetermined delivery dates, quantities, and conditions for materials within a specific time period. Scheduling agreements support continuous raw material supply, predictable demand patterns, and just-in-time delivery requirements while minimizing inventory costs.

In SAP S/4HANA, scheduling agreements support continuous raw material supply, predictable demand patterns, and just-in-time delivery requirements while minimizing inventory costs

Key Benefits:

  • Fiori Apps Integration: Streamlined user experience with "Manage Scheduling Agreements" app 
  • Real-time Analytics: Enhanced reporting capabilities with embedded analytics
  • Simplified Processing: Improved MRP integration and automated release creation 
  • Mobile Capabilities: On-the-go approval and monitoring functionality

Key Links:

SAP Best Practices: Scheduling Agreements in Procurement (Scope Item BMR)

SAP Help link: Procurement Using Scheduling Agreements

 

Types of Scheduling Agreements

1. With Release Documentation (LPA)

Purpose: Provides controlled communication of delivery schedules to suppliers with formal release documentation

S/4HANA Features: 

  • Enhanced release creation profiles with automated MRP integration
  • Real-time delivery schedule transmission capabilities
  • Improved audit trail and compliance tracking

Common Applications: Critical raw materials like wood chips, chemicals, and packaging materials where precise timing and documentation are essential

Key Functions:

  • Enables creation of Forecast (FRC) delivery schedules for medium to long-term planning
  • Supports Just-in-Time (JIT) delivery schedules for short-term requirements
  • Maintains complete audit trail of all communications sent to suppliers

2. Without Release Documentation (LP)

Purpose: Direct transmission of delivery schedules to suppliers without intermediate release steps

S/4HANA Features: 

  • Immediate schedule transmission capabilities
  • Simplified processing workflows in Fiori apps 
  • Enhanced supplier portal integration

Common Applications: Standard consumables and maintenance materials with stable demand patterns

Key Functions:

  • Immediate transmission of schedule changes to suppliers
  • Simplified process for routine materials procurement
  • Reduced administrative overhead for non-critical items

3. Standard Scheduling Agreement

Purpose: Combines flexible delivery scheduling with standardized procurement processes

S/4HANA Features: 

  • Integration with central contracts for pricing 
  • Plant-specific delivery requirements
  • Enhanced supplier collaboration tools

Common Applications: Multi-location operations requiring standardized processes with localized delivery

Key Functions:

  • Leverages master agreement pricing conditions
  • Enables location-specific delivery scheduling
  • Maintains consistency across multiple plants

 

Quota Arrangements

Quota arrangement in scheduling agreements allows paper companies to distribute procurement requirements among multiple suppliers based on predefined allocation percentages. This ensures supply security, competitive pricing, and risk mitigation by avoiding single-source dependencies for critical materials.

S/4HANA Enhancements:

  • Simplified Configuration: Streamlined quota arrangement setup through Fiori apps
  • Real-time Allocation: Dynamic quota calculation and distribution
  • Performance Integration: Supplier performance metrics influence quota allocation

Configuration Elements

  • Quota Base: Percentage or quantity-based allocation among qualified suppliers
  • Quota Period: Typically aligned with production planning cycles (monthly, quarterly)
  • Quota Allocation: Automatic distribution logic that considers supplier capacity, performance, and strategic relationships

 

Scaling in Scheduling Agreements

S/4HANA Scaling Features:

  • Automated Calculations: Real-time scale application during order processing
  • Flexible Scale Types: Multiple scaling methodologies supported
  • Integration with Contracts: Centralized scale management
  • Condition Management: Enhanced pricing condition handling

 

Just-in-Time (JIT) Integration

JIT Delivery Concepts

  • JIT Call-offs: Short-term delivery schedules (daily or hourly) that signal suppliers to start production and deliver materials precisely when needed 
  • Delivery Schedules: Frequent communication of requirements, typically weekly for forecast schedules and daily for JIT schedules
  • Kanban Integration: Direct connection with production planning to trigger material replenishment based on actual consumption

SAP S/4HANA JIT Enhancements:

  • Real-time Integration: Seamless connection with SAP PP and advanced planning systems
  • Mobile Capabilities: Supplier access via mobile devices and Fiori launchpad
  • Predictive Analytics: AI-driven demand forecasting integration
  • Supplier Collaboration: Enhanced portal integration with real-time visibility

JIT Configuration Parameters

  • Delivery Tolerance: Typically +5%/-0% for critical materials to prevent production disruptions
  • Lead Time: Varies by material type - chemicals (2-3 days), packaging (1 week), raw materials (2-4 weeks)
  • Safety Stock: Minimal buffer inventory maintained to support true JIT operations

 

Key Takeaways

  • SAP S/4HANA scheduling agreements transform procurement through enhanced automation, real-time analytics, and streamlined supplier collaboration, reducing administrative overhead while improving delivery performance
  • Successful implementation requires careful attention to document types (LPA vs LP), proper master data configuration, and alignment with MRP processes to maximize JIT delivery capabilities
  • The integration of Fiori apps, mobile functionality, and embedded analytics in S/4HANA elevates scheduling agreements from basic procurement tools to strategic supplier relationship enablers

entry.title: Re: Backorder Processing in aATP - SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
Hello Nipun,

I am creating a very simple WIN, REDISTRIBUTE and LOSE strategy scenario. I used Configure BOP Segment and created 3 strategies using Delivery Priority 01, 02, 03 as Selection Conditions for Sales Documents in WIN, REDISTRIBUTE and LOSE strategy whereas Sort Conditions is Record Creation date with blank  Custom Sort Sequence. Now created a Variant using the Fiori App Configure BOP Variant. Now for a Material, I have total stock as 8 PC's. I created 1st Sales Order with 5 PC's and kept delivery Priority as 03 which got confirmed. Now created 2nd Sales Order with 3 PC's with Delivery Priority 02 and this also got confirmed. Now 3rd Order is created with 6 PC's with Delivery Priority 01 and this got confirmed with 0 PC's as there is no stock now. Now in Configure BOP Variant I pressed the Simulation button and clicked on Show Last Runs button which shows below result-
1st Sales Order : From 5 PC's => 0 PC's which is correct as this will follow LOSE Strategy (Delivery Priority - 03)

3rd Sales Order : From 0 PC's => 6 PC's this also is correct as this will follow WIN Strategy (Delivery Priority - 01)

2nd Sales Order : From 3 PC's => 0 PC's this is not correct as this will follow REDISTRIBUT Strategy and the qty should become from 3 PC's => 2 PC's (Delivery Priority - 02).
Total Stock 8 PC's should be consumed by 3rd Sales Order (6 PC's) and 2nd Sales Order (2 PC's).
Request you to please have a look on my scenario and let me know where I am doing the mistake ?
Note : If I look your screenshots, in that WIN is becoming 0 =>9, LOSE is becoming 8 => 0 and REDISTRIBUTE is becoming 4 => 3.

Indranil Chatterjee

entry.title: Request for standard production reporting apps
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,

We would like to request your support to identify standard SAP Fiori reporting applications in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that provide simplified and user-friendly production reporting, especially for process manufacturing scenarios.

Currently, the COOISPI report is available in the system; however, business users find it complex and difficult to interpret, particularly for day-to-day operational analysis. For this reason, the users are looking for more intuitive reporting applications, similar in concept to the informative and analytical apps available in the QM module, but focused on production execution.

The main business requirements are to:

  • Easily visualize total quantities produced over a given period
  • Optionally compare total produced quantities vs Planned Independent Requirements (PIRs)
  • Access production information in a summary and user-friendly format, without having to navigate order by order

The focus is informational repor

entry.title: Re: (-1) DBM Transaction is auto rolled back by detecting...
entry.content:
Hello,

The error has come because there is an error in the Transaction Notification. The issue is that when using Report and Layout Manager, the TN error is not shown on the front-end. It simply gives the error you have mentioned (at least till FP2602).

My troubleshooting led me to check the TN on User Master Data (object_type = 12). My guess is that when you import a new report, each User Master Data record has some update. So, the TN with object_type 12 is run for every USERID. If any of the records gives an error, the entire transaction (addition of report) gets rolled back.

Instead of turning off the TN, I suggest you troubleshoot which TN is giving an error.

I have also raised an incident with SAP requesting the TN error to be shown on the front-end in System Messages Log.

entry.title: Re: Multiple FI documents from a single reconciliation key ?
entry.content:
Additional information.

If fields DFKKOP-XEIPH & DFKKOPK-XEIPH are checked in the FICA document, then separate line items are formed within a single FIGL document during forward posting.

If field DFKKKO-XEIBH is checked in the FICA document, then separate FIGL documents are created during forward posting with FICA document number in the reference field of the FIGL document.
